items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment document is available
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Previous inspection reports are checked
Crane manufacturer name, address, serial number and power rates are marked or tagged
Operator is certified and licensed
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Crane is painted safety yellow
Crane is painted safety yellow-and-black stripes on offshore facilities
Rated capacity of crane is marked (Arabic & English)
Is erection and dismantling procedures are based on manufacturer recommendations
A durable rating chart, which is legible, is visible to the operator at the controls
Main structure components are free of defects (bolts, pins, mast sections for cracks, bent structural members, etc.)
Flags and/or markers with high visibility are placed on guys and visible to the operator
Remote control operation is available
Crane stops when remote control signal (for any crane motion) is lost
Access ladders to cab are provided (platform surfaces to be skid resistant)
Foot walks and ladders are provided where appropriate (foot walks to be minimum of 18" wide, with slip resistant surface)
Controls are within reach of the operator (stop, start, clutch, gear select, engine speed and emergency stop - when internal combustion engine is the main power source)
Cab door is restrained from moving during crane operation or travel
Electrical equipment are protected from the environment and guarded
Electrical equipment can be mains disconnected at ground level (in case of electrically operated cranes)
Load trolley can brake in travel mode (automatically in case of power loss and drive rope breakage)
Afire extinguisher is provided in the cab (minimum rating is 10 BC)
Exhaust piping is guarded or insulated (where personnel could contact it)
Fuel filler pipes are located or protected so as to avoid spillage
guards are fitted to exposed moving parts and can support 136 kg without deformation
Clutch is in good condition and protected against weather and oil contaminations
Limiting devices are fully operational for trolley travel, boom luffing, upper hoist limit, crane travel, lifted load and radius
Are indicating devices calibrated (if required)
Are load chart's letters and figures legible and attached in a location visible to the operator while at the controls
Slewing (swing) motion of the crane is smooth for both start and stop
Slewing brakes are operational and can be set to hold the position without further operator action
Counterweight values are correct
Counterweight is fixed in place and (for movable weights uncontrolled movement is not possible)
Welded members/Joints are free of defects (cracks, corrosion, etc.)
A metal receptacle is in place for storage of small hand tools and lubricating equipment (in cab or machinery housing)
Brakes are protected from weather and other industrial liquids
HOISTING UNIT Remaining rope on the drum (load hoist and/or boom hoist) in the 2.1.1 extreme low position is at least 3 full wraps

BOOM AND JIB For standing ropes, the minimum design factor shall be 3.0. Fiber core 2.2.1 ropes with swaged fittings and rotation-resistant ropes shall not be used for pendants, stay ropes, or guy ropes poured sockets or swaged sockets (pendant ropes) are in good 2.2.2 condition

MAST STRUCTURE No determined hazard on foundations and structural supports for 2.3.1 settlement, movement, deformations, accumulation of standing water or debris, or other signs of abnormal conditions. 2.3.2 Travel rails are level and secure 2.3.3 Rails are grounded (for electrically powered cranes) Stops or buffers are correctly adjusted for simultaneous contact with both 2.3.4 sides of the travel base (stops to be fitted not less that 1 meter inboard of last rail support) 2.3.5 Rail sweeps are in good condition Means to limit drop of a travel truck are in place and in good condition (in 2.3.6 case of axle or wheel failure) 2.3.7 Truck wheels are guarded 2.3.8 Travel brakes can lock the wheels 2.3.9 Stairs/access ladders are secure and in good condition

CABIN 2.4.1 Control levers and/or pedals functions are labeled Engine exhaust gases are piped and discharged away from the operator 2.4.2 cab 2.4.3 Operator cab has a safety glazing glass Cab lighting (artificial or natural) is sufficient to enable the operator to 2.4.4 observe the controls 2.4.5 Operator cab audible signal devices are operable and in good condition
SLEWING COMPONENTS 2.5.1 Lubrication points are accessible
TROLLEY Trolley can not become detached from its guide rails (in case of axle or 2.6.1 wheel breakage)

POWER PLANT AND ELECTRICALS 2.7.1 Engine speed control is working satisfactorily 2.7.2 Transmission selector can be operated satisfactorily 2.7.3 Engine emergency stop switch is working properly 2.7.4 Pedals and hand levers are easily operated and well functioning Electrical crane has a main disconnect switch at or near the initial base 2.7.5 of the crane Electrical equipment is so located or guarded that live parts are not 2.7.6 exposed to inadvertent contact under normal operating conditions Electrical equipment is protected from dirt, grease, oil, moisture and 2.7.7 other weather conditions 2.7.8 An overload device is in place for each motor 2.7.9 Lighting protection is in place 2.7.10 Resistor units are supported to minimize vibrations 2.7.11 Resistor parts are not suffering from corrosion 2.7.12 An indication light is in place to magnet is energized (if magnet is used)

HOOKS 2.8.1 Labeling and manufacturer data are available and legible 2.8.2 Safe working load is clearly marked on the hook 2.8.3 Hook is not bent or twisted Hook is not distorted in the throat opening 2.8.4 • Max. allowable throat opening is 5% not to exceed 1∕4 in. (6 mm) or as recommended by the manufacturer. Maximum wear in the hook bowl is not exceeding 10% (compared to 2.8.5 new hook) or as per manufacturer recommendations 2.8.6 Hook is not cracked, gouged or shows nicks 2.8.7 Hook can lock (if it is a self-locking hook) Inoperative latch (if provided) — any damaged latch or malfunctioning 2.8.8 latch that does not close the hook’s throat
